tab-ai-instant-context-a1.task:
Eliminate the ~3.4s delay when pressing Tab to open inline AI chat in the main menu. The delay is caused by build_tab_ai_context_cards() in src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs (around line 235) calling capture_context_snapshot() with include_focused_window: true, which triggers capture_focused_window_screenshot() in src/platform/ai_commands.rs — this enumerates all windows via xcap, captures a screenshot, resizes with Lanczos3, and encodes PNG, all synchronously on the main thread. The context cards only need the window TITLE, not a screenshot.

FIX: Extend the existing frontmost_app_tracker (src/frontmost_app_tracker/mod.rs) to also cache the focused window title. The tracker already watches NSWorkspaceDidActivateApplicationNotification and stores TrackedApp {pid, bundle_id, name} in a global RwLock<TrackerState>. Add a window_title: Option<String> field to TrackedApp. On each app activation notification, also grab the frontmost window title via a lightweight method — either CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo (fast, ~1ms) filtering by the activated app's PID, or AXUIElementCopyAttributeValue for kAXFocusedWindowAttribute then kAXTitleAttribute. Do NOT use xcap or capture screenshots.

Then update build_tab_ai_context_cards() in src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs to use get_last_real_app() from the tracker instead of calling capture_context_snapshot() with include_focused_window: true. Replace the expensive snapshot call with the instant tracker lookup. The frontmost app card should use app.name and the focused window card should use app.window_title (if Some and non-empty).

Also update the two other call sites that use capture_context_snapshot with include_focused_window: true — check capture_focused_window_live() in src/context_snapshot/capture.rs to see if it can also benefit from using the cached title instead of the expensive xcap path (at minimum for the title-only use case).

KEY FILES: src/frontmost_app_tracker/mod.rs (TrackedApp struct, TrackerState, the NSWorkspace observer callback around line 398, start_tracking(), get_last_real_app()), src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs (build_tab_ai_context_cards around line 221, open_tab_ai_chat around line 135), src/context_snapshot/capture.rs (capture_focused_window_live around line 182, capture_context_snapshot around line 206), src/platform/ai_commands.rs (capture_focused_window_screenshot — do NOT modify this, it may still be needed for actual screenshot use cases).

CONSTRAINTS from CLAUDE.md: Use parking_lot::Mutex/RwLock (non-poisoning). Every unsafe block needs a // SAFETY: comment. ObjC runtime is objc = 0.2 (NOT objc2). Use msg_send! with explicit return types. Nil-check ObjC pointers. Every #[cfg(target_os = macos)] function needs a #[cfg(not(target_os = macos))] stub. Use tracing::{info,warn,debug} for logging. No unwrap/expect in production code. Use ? + anyhow::Context or explicit handling.

VERIFICATION: cargo check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test
tab-ai-instant-context-a1.notes: [auto] timed out after 1800s — task too broad

1. src/builtins/mod.rs — Add ClaudeCode variant to UtilityCommandType enum. Register a new built-in entry near QuickTerminal:
   entries.push(BuiltInEntry::new_with_icon("builtin-claude-code", "Claude Code", "Open Claude Code in an instant terminal session", vec!["claude", "code", "terminal", "cli", "repl", "anthropic"], BuiltInFeature::UtilityCommand(UtilityCommandType::ClaudeCode), "🤖"));

2. src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs — Add match arm for UtilityCommandType::ClaudeCode in the existing UtilityCommand match block. Pattern after QuickTerminal: set self.opened_from_main_menu = true, call self.open_claude_code_terminal(cx), return Self::builtin_success(dctx, "open_claude_code_terminal").

3. src/app_execute/utility_views.rs — Add a new method open_claude_code_terminal that creates a shell command string: zsh -lc 'mkdir -p "$HOME/.scriptkit/sessions" && cd "$HOME/.scriptkit/sessions" && exec claude' and passes it to self.open_terminal_with_command(command, cx). Pattern after existing open_quick_terminal or similar helpers in this file.

Key anchors: UtilityCommandType enum in builtins/mod.rs, execute_builtin_inner() match in builtin_execution.rs, open_terminal_with_command() helper in utility_views.rs. AppView::QuickTerminalView is reused — no new AppView variant needed.

terminal-keyboard-overhaul-a1.task:
Overhaul the embedded terminal keyboard handling, footer, and escape behavior for full TUI compatibility. This is a Rust GPUI app with an embedded alacritty_terminal. The terminal key handler is in src/term_prompt/mod.rs, the terminal render wrapper is in src/render_prompts/term.rs, and the terminal handle is in src/terminal/alacritty/handle_runtime.rs.

CHANGES NEEDED (implement ALL):

1. PRINTABLE-TEXT ALLOWLIST (src/term_prompt/mod.rs ~line 1008):
   Currently we skip key_char for enter/return/tab/backspace. Oracle recommended flipping the model: only use key_char for printable text input. Replace the skip-list check with: use key_char ONLY when key_char exists AND key_char bytes are all >= 0x20 (space) AND the key name is NOT a known special key. All other keys go through the special key encoder. This prevents future bugs where GPUI's key_char sends wrong bytes for special keys.

2. MODIFIER-AWARE KEY ENCODER (src/term_prompt/mod.rs, in the special key match block ~line 1025):
   Add support for modified key variants using standard xterm/CSI sequences:
   - Shift+Tab → ESC[Z (reverse tab / backtab)
   - Alt/Meta + printable char → ESC prefix + char (e.g. Alt+b = ESC b)
   - Ctrl+Left/Right → ESC[1;5D / ESC[1;5C (word navigation)
   - Ctrl+Up/Down → ESC[1;5A / ESC[1;5B
   - Shift+arrows → ESC[1;2A/B/C/D
   - Alt+arrows → ESC[1;3A/B/C/D
   - Ctrl+Delete → ESC[3;5~
   - Ctrl+Home/End → ESC[1;5H / ESC[1;5F
   - Alt+Backspace → ESC + DEL (0x1b 0x7f)
   The modifier parameter encoding for CSI sequences is: 1+modifier_bits where shift=1, alt=2, ctrl=4, meta=8. So Ctrl = ;5, Shift = ;2, Alt = ;3, Ctrl+Shift = ;6, etc.

3. TERMINAL-SPECIFIC FOOTER (src/render_prompts/term.rs):
   Replace the current render_universal_prompt_hint_strip() call (~line 306) with a terminal-specific footer that shows ONLY '⌘W Close'. The terminal should have full keyboard control — no Enter/Tab/Actions hints that conflict with terminal usage. Create a simple inline hint strip with just the close shortcut. Use the same visual style (text_xs, hint opacity from the theme) as the universal strip but with just one hint.

4. ESCAPE PASSTHROUGH (src/render_prompts/term.rs ~line 176-198):
   Currently Escape is intercepted: for SDK terminals it's blocked, for QuickTerminal it calls go_back_or_close(). Change this: when the terminal is in QuickTerminalView, Escape should ONLY be handled by the outer wrapper if Cmd is held (Cmd+W closes). Plain Escape must pass through to the terminal so TUI apps (vim, claude, htop) receive it. Remove the is_key_escape check for QuickTerminalView. Keep the non-dismissable escape blocking for SDK TermPrompt (those terminals are script-controlled and should not close on Escape).

5. CMD+W ONLY CLOSE (src/render_prompts/term.rs):
   The Cmd+W close handler already exists at line ~200-204. Keep it. But remove or gate the plain Escape close for QuickTerminalView since we're passing Escape through now.

6. STARTUP COMMAND CR FIX (src/terminal/alacritty/handle_creation.rs ~line 132-141):
   The startup command injection currently appends newline. Check if it appends '\n' and change it to '\r' for consistency with the Enter fix. Terminals expect CR for line submission.

After all changes, run: cargo check && cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings && cargo nextest run --lib (with 120s timeout per command). Fix any compilation or lint errors.
terminal-keyboard-overhaul-a1.notes: done. exit=0. final=.ai/logs/terminal-keyboard-overhaul-a1.final.md. See final message file.

report.taskSummary: Implemented the embedded terminal keyboard, footer, escape passthrough, and startup CR handling overhaul for full TUI compatibility and verified it with the required Rust checks.

remove-legacy-tab-ai-a1.task:
Remove all dead/legacy TabAiChat code from the codebase. The Tab AI surface was pivoted from an inline chat (TabAiChat) to a harness terminal (QuickTerminalView) in commit 97a4b505. The legacy code is now dead and must be fully excised.

WHAT TO REMOVE:
1. In src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs: delete open_tab_ai_full_view_chat(), build_tab_ai_context_cards(), resolve_tab_ai_clipboard_context(), submit_tab_ai_chat_with_intent(), submit_tab_ai_chat(), handle_tab_ai_chat_key_down(), close_tab_ai_chat(), and any helper only used by those functions. Remove the 'Legacy full-view chat submission path' comment block added in 97a4b505.
2. In src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs: delete the TabAiChat variant from AppView enum, delete the TabAiChat struct and all its impl blocks (Render, Focusable, new(), current_intent(), selected_suggestion(), move_selected_suggestion(), restore_target(), etc.), delete FocusTarget::TabAiChat variant.
3. In src/main_sections/app_state.rs: delete the tab_ai_task field.
4. In src/main_sections/render_impl.rs: delete the AppView::TabAiChat match arm in the render dispatch.
5. In src/ai/mod.rs: remove re-exports of deleted types (TabAiChat if exported).
6. In src/app_impl/startup_new_tab.rs: remove the 'Block Tab while the legacy inline chat is open' guard that matches AppView::TabAiChat.
7. In tests/tab_ai_routing.rs: remove tests that reference deleted code (legacy_inline_chat_retained_but_not_primary, legacy_close_tab_ai_chat_still_exists, legacy_tab_ai_chat_stores_preview_desktop_snapshot, escape_in_tab_ai_chat_calls_close, close_tab_ai_chat_restores_return_view_and_focus, empty_input_enter_submits_selected_suggestion, up_down_arrows_cycle_suggestions_on_empty_input, submit_rejects_implicit_target_intent_without_focused_target, open_captures_preview_once_and_passes_to_entity, tab_ai_chat_stores_preview_desktop_snapshot, submit_uses_stored_preview_snapshot_not_recapture). Also remove any include_str constants that are no longer referenced.
8. Chase ALL compile errors from removed types/variants — every match arm, every field access, every import. The Rust compiler will tell you what's broken.

KEY FILES: src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs, src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs, src/main_sections/app_state.rs, src/main_sections/render_impl.rs, src/app_impl/startup_new_tab.rs, src/ai/mod.rs, tests/tab_ai_routing.rs, src/render_prompts/tab_ai_chat.rs (if exists)

VERIFICATION GATE (mandatory before reporting done):
  cargo check && cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings && cargo nextest run --lib
Also run: cargo nextest run --test tab_ai_routing

DO NOT remove: open_tab_ai_harness_terminal, close_tab_ai_harness_terminal, ensure_tab_ai_harness_terminal, inject_tab_ai_harness_submission, build_tab_ai_context_from, snapshot_tab_ai_ui, TabAiContextBlob, TabAiUiSnapshot, or any harness/context types. Those are the LIVE code path.

IMPORTANT: This is a Rust codebase using include!() macros. Files in src/app_impl/, src/main_sections/, src/render_prompts/ share the main.rs namespace. There are no top-level use statements in those files. Read CLAUDE.md for compilation context rules.

dictation-capture-a1.task:
Create the entire src/dictation/ module tree for a headless audio capture runtime. This is greenfield — no existing dictation code exists yet.

FILES TO CREATE (all under src/dictation/):
- mod.rs: module facade with pub mod capture; mod device; mod types; mod visualizer; and pub use re-exports for all public types/functions
- types.rs: DictationDeviceId(String), DictationDeviceInfo{id,name,is_default}, DictationCaptureConfig with Default (16000 Hz, 40ms chunks, 60ms level window), RawAudioChunk{sample_rate_hz,channels,samples:Vec<f32>}, CapturedAudioChunk{sample_rate_hz,samples:Vec<f32>,duration:Duration}, DictationLevel{rms:f32,peak:f32}, DictationCaptureEvent enum (Chunk/Level/EndOfStream)
- visualizer.rs: compute_level(samples:&[f32])->DictationLevel (RMS+peak with clamping), bars_for_level(DictationLevel)->[f32;9] (symmetric bell shape, clamped 0.08..1.0)
- capture.rs: Pure-Rust helpers mix_to_mono, resample_linear, normalize_chunk, run_processor. Plus macOS AVFoundation start_capture/DictationCaptureHandle following src/camera/mod.rs lifecycle pattern exactly (session/delegate/queue/sender ownership, Drop stops session, drains queue with dispatch_sync_f, nulls sender ivar, reclaims box, releases ObjC objects, joins processor thread). DictationCaptureHandle struct with unsafe impl Send. extract_pcm_chunk supporting float32 and int16 PCM only. Audio delegate callback only does extract_pcm_chunk + send RawAudioChunk.
- device.rs: list_input_devices()->Result<Vec<DictationDeviceInfo>> and default_input_device()->Result<Option<DictationDeviceInfo>> using AVCaptureDevice/AVMediaTypeAudio on macOS, empty stubs on other platforms. Use objc 0.2 crate (NOT objc2). Use c"" string literals for ObjC interop.
- tests.rs: Unit tests for mix_to_mono, resample_linear, normalize_chunk, compute_level, bars_for_level. Referenced via #[cfg(test)] mod tests; in mod.rs.

ALSO MODIFY:
- src/main.rs: Add 'mod dictation;' declaration (this file uses include!() pattern — check where other mod declarations live, likely near top with other mod statements)

KEY ANCHORS:
- src/camera/mod.rs — THE template for AVFoundation lifecycle (CaptureHandle ownership, delegate callback, Drop cleanup). Port this pattern exactly for audio.
- src/main.rs — where mod declarations live
- CLAUDE.md rules: use objc 0.2, SAFETY comments on unsafe blocks, c"" literals, #[cfg(target_os=macos)] with not(target_os=macos) stubs, no unwrap in non-test code, use anyhow::Context for errors, pub(crate) default visibility

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:
- capture.rs pure-Rust helpers (mix_to_mono, resample_linear, normalize_chunk, run_processor) must be pub(crate) so tests.rs can import them
- Use async_channel::bounded(128) for event channel, std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel(128) for raw channel
- Use std::thread::spawn for processor thread (not cx.spawn — this is outside GPUI)
- AVMediaTypeAudio is NSString 'soun' not a constant import
- CMSampleBufferGetAudioBufferListWithRetainedBlockBuffer for PCM extraction
- Support only float32 and int16 PCM formats in extract_pcm_chunk
- No cpal dependency. Pure AVFoundation.
- run_processor sends Level then Chunk for each raw buffer

EXIT CRITERIA: cargo check && cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings && cargo nextest run --lib must all pass

report.taskSummary: Created a new headless dictation capture module with AVFoundation audio capture, processing helpers, device enumeration, visualizer utilities, tests, and module wiring, then verified, committed, and pushed it.

device-model-readiness-a1.task:
Confirm and stabilize microphone selection persistence and Whisper model readiness on the integrated dictation path. This is iteration 12 of an ongoing dictation migration from vercel-voice into Script Kit GPUI.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. A valid saved selectedDeviceId is resolved and used; a missing saved device falls back to the system default; a stale saved device is cleared without blocking dictation start.
2. list_input_device_menu_items(...) and apply_device_selection(...) behave consistently with the persisted settings shape under dictation.selectedDeviceId in ScriptKitUserPreferences (src/config/types.rs).
3. resolve_default_model_path() and WhisperDictationEngine::new(&DictationTranscriptionConfig::default()) either initialize successfully with a valid model or return a surfaced error for a missing/invalid model, and silent input still resolves to Ok(None).

KEY FILES:
- src/dictation/device.rs — mic enumeration, resolve_selected_input_device, build_device_menu_items, list_input_device_menu_items, apply_device_selection, save_dictation_device_id
- src/dictation/runtime.rs — toggle_dictation, resolve_preferred_device (loads pref, clears stale), transcribe_captured_audio (lazy Whisper init)
- src/dictation/transcription.rs — WhisperDictationEngine::new (validates model path), resolve_default_model_path, DictationTranscriber, DictationTranscriptionConfig
- src/config/types.rs — DictationPreferences { selected_device_id: Option<String> }, ScriptKitUserPreferences
- src/config/loader.rs — load_user_preferences, save_user_preferences (merge strategy preserving unknown keys)
- src/dictation/tests.rs — existing test suite (~85KB, 70+ tests)

ARCHITECTURE ANCHORS:
- DictationPreferences lives at dictation.selectedDeviceId in settings.json (camelCase serde)
- resolve_preferred_device() in runtime.rs loads prefs, calls list_input_devices + resolve_selected_input_device, and clears stale prefs via save_dictation_device_id(None)
- WhisperDictationEngine::new validates model_path exists and is a regular file (lazy load on first transcribe)
- Default model path: get_kit_path()/models/whisper-medium-q4_1.bin
- Settings persistence uses save_user_preferences which does JSON merge to preserve unknown keys

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT add cpal; stay on AVFoundation capture path
- Do NOT add settings UI; only stabilize persistence and fallback behavior
- Do NOT use unwrap()/expect() in production code
- Use ? + anyhow::Context for error handling
- All tests must pass: cargo check && cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings && cargo nextest run --lib
- If tests already pass with no changes needed, verify and confirm that each acceptance criterion is met by the existing code

APPROACH: Read each key file carefully. Verify the device selection persistence round-trip (save -> load -> resolve). Verify stale device fallback clears the pref. Verify WhisperDictationEngine::new returns proper errors for missing/invalid model. Verify silent input returns Ok(None). Add or fix tests if any acceptance criterion is not covered. Fix any bugs found. Run the full verification gate.
device-model-readiness-a1.notes: [policy] commits touch files outside declared scope

report.taskSummary: Stabilized integrated dictation so silent input skips Whisper initialization, preserved and regression-tested selected microphone persistence behavior, and verified default/missing model-path readiness handling.

keyboard-shortcut-consistency-a1.task:
Implement keyboard shortcut rendering consistency fixes across Script Kit GPUI. This is a Rust GPUI app.

CONTEXT: We just added SVG keyboard glyph icons (return.svg, tab.svg, command.svg in assets/icons/) and a parse_hint() function in src/components/hint_strip.rs that converts Unicode glyph prefixes to SVG icons. Several surfaces are migrated but parse_hint() only handles ↵/⏎/↩, ⌘, and Tab. Multi-key chords like ⌘⇧↵ render as command-icon + raw text '⇧↵'.

TASKS (in order):
1. Copy shift.svg and escape.svg from ~/dev/zed/assets/icons/ into assets/icons/. Change stroke='black' to stroke='currentColor' in both. Add CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR external path constants for them in src/components/hint_strip.rs alongside the existing RETURN_ICON_PATH, TAB_ICON_PATH, COMMAND_ICON_PATH.

2. Expand parse_hint() in src/components/hint_strip.rs to handle:
   - ⇧ (U+21E7 Shift) → shift.svg icon
   - Esc prefix → text-only keycap (no SVG needed, just render 'Esc' as a styled keycap element)
   - Multi-glyph prefixes: when a hint starts with multiple modifier glyphs (e.g. '⌘⇧↵'), parse ALL of them into separate icons before the label text. The current code only matches the first character.

3. Update the HintElement enum to support multiple icons: instead of KeyHint { icon_path, label }, support KeyHint { icon_paths: Vec<&'static str>, label } or similar so ⌘⇧↵ renders as [cmd-icon][shift-icon][return-icon] + 'Send + Context'.

4. For 'Esc' text (no SVG), render it as a small styled text keycap (similar to how Zed renders Key { key, color, size } — a small div with text_xs, rounded corners, and muted background).

VERIFICATION GATE (mandatory before reporting success):
cargo check --lib && cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings

KEY FILES:
- src/components/hint_strip.rs — the parse_hint() function and render_hint_element_hsla()
- src/components/mod.rs — exports render_hint_icons, render_hint_icons_hsla
- src/icons/types/embedded.rs — EmbeddedIcon enum (has Return, Tab, Command variants)
- assets/icons/ — SVG icon files
- ~/dev/zed/assets/icons/shift.svg and escape.svg — source SVGs to copy

CONSTRAINTS:
- SVGs MUST use stroke='currentColor' not stroke='black'
- MUST use svg().external_path() with CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR absolute paths (not svg().path())
- Do NOT modify any files outside hint_strip.rs, embedded.rs, mod.rs, and the new SVG assets unless strictly necessary
- Run: cargo check --lib && cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings
keyboard-shortcut-consistency-a1.notes: done. exit=0. final=.ai/logs/keyboard-shortcut-consistency-a1.final.md. See final message file.

report.taskSummary: Added consistent multi-key keyboard shortcut hint parsing and rendering with Shift and Esc support, plus new keyboard glyph assets.

vibrancy-fixes-a1.task:
Fix three opacity/vibrancy issues in the theme chooser. All three are in the same file cluster so do them together.

TASK 1 — Window Opacity slider edits wrong field:
The 'Window Opacity' control in src/render_builtins/theme_chooser_customize_controls.rs (~line 86) changes op.main and op.title_bar, but the actual root blur/backdrop control is op.vibrancy_background. Change the slider's on_click handler to set op.vibrancy_background = value instead of op.main/op.title_bar. Also update the Cmd+-/= keyboard handlers in src/render_builtins/theme_chooser_setup_key.rs and src/render_builtins/theme_chooser.rs to change vibrancy_background instead of main/title_bar. The OPACITY_PRESETS array and find_opacity_preset_index should compare against vibrancy_background, not main.

TASK 2 — Wire VibrancyMaterial to native layer:
src/platform/vibrancy_config.rs has configure_window_vibrancy_material_for_appearance(is_dark: bool) which hardcodes HUD_WINDOW for dark and POPOVER for light (line 84). Add a VibrancyMaterial parameter so the user's material selection takes effect. The VibrancyMaterial enum is in src/theme/types.rs (variants: Hud, Popover, Menu, Sidebar, Content). Map each to its NSVisualEffectMaterial constant. Update all call sites (search for configure_window_vibrancy_material_for_appearance in src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs, src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs, src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs, src/main_entry/runtime_window.rs, src/main_sections/window_visibility.rs) to pass the current theme's vibrancy.material. Also update the non-macOS stub signature.

TASK 3 — Live vibrancy toggle doesn't update native window:
When toggling vibrancy on/off in the theme chooser, the code only calls sync_gpui_component_theme + cx.notify(). It does NOT update the native window's blur state. After the GPUI sync in the vibrancy toggle handlers (in theme_chooser_customize_controls.rs, theme_chooser_setup_key.rs, theme_chooser.rs — search for 'vibrancy.enabled = !'), also call platform::configure_window_vibrancy_material_for_appearance with the current is_dark and material values.

VERIFICATION: Run cargo check && cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings. The project uses include!() files so standard module rules don't apply in those dirs. Tests: cargo nextest run --lib (timeout 120s).

KEY FILES: src/render_builtins/theme_chooser_customize_controls.rs, src/render_builtins/theme_chooser_setup_key.rs, src/render_builtins/theme_chooser.rs, src/platform/vibrancy_config.rs, src/theme/types.rs (VibrancyMaterial enum), src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs, src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs, src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs, src/main_entry/runtime_window.rs, src/main_sections/window_visibility.rs

report.taskSummary: Fixed theme chooser opacity and vibrancy controls so window opacity targets vibrancy_background, native material selection follows the chosen VibrancyMaterial, and live vibrancy toggles/material changes immediately reconfigure the native window.
